subject: Sociology
title: Transnational Identity
description: A quick, practical guide to Transnational Identity.

hook.text: She speaks Mandarin at home, English at work, and Spanish with friends. Which culture is hers? All of them.

buildup.text: Transnational identity means belonging to multiple cultures and nations simultaneously, without choosing just one.

discovery.text: Globalization creates hybrid identities: people blend languages, foods, values, and traditions from multiple worlds.

twist.text: Traditional sociology assumed one identity per person. Transnational lives expose that assumption as outdated.

climax.text: Identity in a global world isn't a single story. It's a mosaic assembled from every place you've called home.

punchline.text: Identity doesn't fit inside one border.

quiz.question: What is transnational identity?
options: Belonging to multiple cultures and nations simultaneously
